suggestions.title: getTeamVibe called 4+ times per render — STILL UNFIXED from review #13
suggestions.description: In App.jsx, getTeamVibe(activeWorkers) is called inline at least 4 times: vibe-pill (line ~header), StatsBar component, favicon effect, and document title effect. Each call filters and recomputes. Fix: compute once with useMemo at the top of App and pass the result as a prop. This is the third time I'm flagging this.

suggestions.title: Office window sky uses viewer's local timezone — STILL UNFIXED from review #12
suggestions.description: WindowElement in Office.jsx uses new Date().getHours() which gives the viewer's local time. A viewer in Tokyo sees Tokyo sky while the office is supposed to be in PST. Use Intl.DateTimeFormat with timeZone: 'America/Los_Angeles' or compute with a fixed UTC offset. This has been flagged THREE reviews in a row now.

suggestions.title: ROLE_EMOJIS, ROLE_COLORS, DEFAULT_ROSTER scattered across files — STILL UNFIXED
suggestions.description: ROLE_EMOJIS lives in App.jsx, ROLE_COLORS and DEFAULT_ROSTER live in Office.jsx. Adding a new role means editing 2-3 files. Extract all role-related constants to src/utils/constants.js. Third time flagging this.

suggestions.title: Tab state not synced with browser back/forward — STILL UNFIXED from review #13
suggestions.description: Clicking tabs updates location.hash via window.location.replace(), but there's no hashchange or popstate listener. Pressing browser Back doesn't switch tabs. Add useEffect with a hashchange event listener that calls setTab. This was flagged last review.

suggestions.title: ActivityLog creates 2 new arrays every render — STILL UNFIXED
suggestions.description: [...entries].reverse().slice(0, 20) in ActivityLog allocates two arrays per render. Wrap in useMemo(). Small fix, growing impact as activity log gets longer.

suggestions.title: No loading skeleton for Office tab
suggestions.description: Dashboard has LoadingSkeleton but the Office tab shows an empty floor during initial fetch. During the first load, the office view looks like a ghost town before data arrives. Add a subtle loading state or skeleton for the office view.

suggestions.title: Keyboard shortcut 1/2 for tabs conflicts with typing in any future input
suggestions.description: The keydown handler checks for e.target.tagName === 'INPUT' || 'TEXTAREA' but doesn't check contenteditable elements or elements with role='textbox'. If any rich text input is added, shortcuts will fire during typing. Also, there's no discoverability — users don't know 1/2/Esc shortcuts exist.

suggestions.title: No 'recently completed' section — repeated suggestion
suggestions.description: Workers that finish just vanish (with the nice fade animation). A collapsed 'Recently Completed' section showing last 3-5 finished workers would give context. Activity log partially covers this but it's mixed with other events.

suggestions.title: Whiteboard could rotate messages like Bloberto's speech bubbles
suggestions.description: The whiteboard shows one static string per vibe state. It would be more engaging if it rotated through 2-3 messages on an interval, similar to how Bloberto's speech bubbles already work. The VIBE_WHITEBOARD map already exists — just make it an array and cycle.

suggestions.title: Activity filter buttons mix aria-pressed with visual tab pattern
suggestions.description: Filter bar uses role='group' with aria-pressed but behaves like segmented tabs. Either use proper role='tablist'/role='tab' with aria-selected, or keep as toggle buttons. Current mix is semantically confusing for screen readers.

suggestions.title: Magic numbers: 45000ms ambient interval, 400ms fade, 1500ms new pulse
suggestions.description: Several timing constants are hardcoded inline in Office.jsx and App.jsx. Extract to named constants at the top of each file (AMBIENT_INTERVAL_MS, FADE_OUT_MS, NEW_PULSE_MS). Makes tuning easier and code more readable.

suggestions.title: Office plants still lack variety — flagged since review #12
suggestions.description: All three plants use similar green palettes. The cactus could use a small pink flower, the left plant could have a different pot color. Purely cosmetic but it's been flagged three times now.
